name: Buckeye State Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank with 6 branches across Ohio offering retail and business banking, online banking, and home equity lending.
description_long:
Buckeye State Bank is a community-focused bank headquartered in Powell, Ohio, with a regional presence spanning central and northwestern Ohio. The bank operates six physical branch locations in De Graff, Powell, Perrysburg, Columbus (Tri-Village), Bainbridge, and Chillicothe, plus an additional ATM in downtown Powell. The bank emphasizes local banking and has been voted best bank by Central Ohio residents, positioning itself as a relationship-focused alternative to larger regional and national institutions.

The bank offers comprehensive banking services for both retail and business customers, including checking and savings accounts, online and mobile banking platforms, cash flow management and treasury services, home equity lines of credit, loan origination, debit cards, check ordering, and ATM access. They provide separate online portals for retail and business banking, with features for account management, transfers, and customer support through online chat. The institution is FDIC-insured, providing deposit protection up to regulatory limits.

Buckeye State Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on local, personalized service and community presence. The bank features testimonials from local leadership (including a reference to a former Ohio State quarterback in a VP role) and highlights customer voting recognition in Columbus. They maintain full-service branch hours including Saturday lobby access at most locations and drive-up service, differentiating from online-only competitors. Their treasury services and business banking offerings suggest a focus on serving small to mid-sized businesses and commercial customers in addition to retail clients.

As a regional community bank, Buckeye State Bank serves customers seeking traditional banking services with personal relationships and local accountability. However, the website provides limited information about specific product terms, rates, fees, minimum balances, or competitive features. Customers interested in this bank should contact branch locations directly for detailed product specifications and pricing, as such information is not disclosed on the public website. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking portal for retail customers
Online banking portal for business customers
Treasury services and cash flow management
Lost or stolen card reporting and replacement
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Six physical branch locations across Ohio with weekend lobby hours (Saturday mornings)
Dual online banking portals for retail and business customers with secure access
Full-service capabilities including home equity lines of credit for home improvement financing
24/7 toll-free customer support line (1-844-225-9265) for account and card services
Business banking services including treasury services and cashflow management tools
Drive-up ATM access at most branch locations for convenient cash withdrawal
cons: Limited geographic footprint (6 branches in Ohio only) restricts accessibility for customers outside service areas
Website lacks specific information on rates, fees, minimum account balances, and product terms
No mention of mobile app functionality or mobile-specific banking features
No information disclosed about loan products beyond home equity lines and general loan application
Limited transparency on business banking services with no product details or eligibility requirements listed
best_for: Central and northwest Ohio residents seeking relationship-based community banking with in-person branch access
Small to mid-sized business owners needing treasury services, cash flow management, and local banking relationships
Homeowners in Ohio looking to access home equity for renovation or project funding
Customers preferring traditional banking with in-person service over purely digital banking solutions
diagnosis: Buckeye State Bank is best for Ohio residents and small businesses seeking community-oriented banking with local branch presence and relationship-focused service. The main caveat is that specific product rates, fees, and terms are not disclosed online, requiring direct contact with branch locations for detailed information before opening an account.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
